india_trends.title: Foreign remittances to Kerala stay resilient despite West Asia crisis
india_trends.summary: Money from non-resident Indians to Kerala remains strong. Banks like Federal Bank and South Indian Bank show growth in deposits. This inflow is vital for Kerala's economy. india_trends.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack indicated interest rates will remain steady for an extended period due to significant economic uncertainty. india_trends.title: West Asia Conflict: Supply chain woes hit smaller brands harder amid inflation
india_trends.summary: Amid ongoing supply chain challenges and the burden of rising operational costs, smaller consumer goods players are feeling the squeeze. This shift is creating a landscape where larger corporations can swiftly adapt, taking advantage of the situation to broaden their market influence.

india_trends.title: Semaglutide race: Torrent grabs 38% market share
india_trends.summary: Over a dozen Indian companies have launched generic versions of the GLP1 drug used for diabetes and weight management since the expiry of Novo Nordisk's patent for the innovator molecule in end-March. They generated about ₹44 crore in generic semaglutide sales in April.

india_trends.title: Railways plans AI cameras; new orders on hold
india_trends.summary: Research Designs and Standards Organisation first specified internet protocol-based video surveillance systems for rolling stock in March 2018. The norms have since been amended seven times, most recently in June 2025. The current specifications define parameters for closed-circuit television camera

india_trends.title: Costlier palm, soy oils push Indian consumers towards mustard oil
india_trends.summary: India sees a shift towards mustard oil. Rising global prices of palm and soybean oils, influenced by the West Asia conflict and biofuel demand, make imported oils expensive. This trend is further amplified by a depreciating rupee. Record domestic mustard production supports this change.

india_trends.title: Indian banks activate BCPs, shift West Asia staff to India amid Iran-US conflict
india_trends.summary: Indian banks are taking steps to ensure the safety of their staff in West Asia. Some banks have already moved operations to India to manage wholesale activities remotely. This comes after the government advised banks to assess risks and provide support. The situation in the region is being closely m

india_trends.title: Emami to acquire majority stake in personal care co IncNut Digital for ₹321 cr
india_trends.summary: Emami is acquiring a 60% stake in IncNut Digital, the company behind Vedix and SkinKraft. This deal is worth ₹321 crore. Emami will buy the remaining stake later. The acquisition aims to boost Emami's presence in personalized beauty. IncNut's founder believes this partnership will accelerate innovat

india_trends.title: India’s new safe harbour rules create fresh tax dilemma for multinationals, GCCs
india_trends.summary: Multinationals are reassessing their tax strategies. A new safe harbour regime offers lower margins for IT and ITeS services. india_trends.title: Streaming firms eye one-stop shops to build scale and profitability
india_trends.summary: Amazon's integration of MX Player with Prime Video signals a trend towards unified streaming platforms in India. india_trends.title: MoSPI asks states to shift GSDP base year to 2022-23 for better economic data accuracy
india_trends.summary: The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation has issued new guidelines for calculating Gross State Domestic Product. india_trends.summary: The Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme has successfully created jobs for over 36 lakh individuals. india_trends.title: Textile industry seeks removal of cotton import duty
india_trends.summary: India's textile sector faces challenges from high cotton prices and declining production. The industry is urging the government to cut import duties and create a strategic cotton reserve. Improved credit access is also crucial for mills. india_trends.summary: Citigroup unveiled ambitious profitability targets for 2027-2028, aiming for 11-13% adjusted return on tangible common equity, with further growth expected by 2031. india_trends.title: US market today: Blackstone, BlackRock cut value of their private credit funds
india_trends.summary: Blackstone and BlackRock asset management firms reported declines in their private credit fund values for the first quarter. india_trends.title: India's 6.6% growth "very good record" amid global turmoil: Amitabh Kant
india_trends.summary: India leads global economic growth with a 6.6% GDP expansion amidst international turmoil. Former NITI Aayog CEO Amitabh Kant highlights structural reforms and infrastructure as key drivers. india_trends.title: US stocks today: Paine Schwartz-backed Suja Life's shares fall 14% in Nasdaq debut
india_trends.summary: Suja Life, an organic juice maker, saw its shares drop 14.3 percent on its Nasdaq debut. The company's valuation settled at $695.3 million. india_trends.title: Trai proposes fine up to Rs 50 lakh on telcos for improper handling of customer complaints
india_trends.summary: Telecom regulator TRAI is proposing a new system for telcos to handle customer complaints. Service providers must offer clear complaint registration on their web portals, apps, and chatbots. Regular updates on actions taken will be mandatory.
